title: pilosella brachiata subsp. zittaviensis
intro: Pilosella brachiata subsp. zittaviensis, also known as Zittav's alpine dandelion or Zittav's hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Pilosella brachiata subsp. zittaviensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusions, and tinctures, which have been used to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms associated with digestive issues and other health conditions.
